Drug Response Figure Update (8.9.23)

For Ola and Pembro plot from HRD vs HRP slide /96 well samples, make the axis and box lines thicker

ED75 normalized by subtracting DMSO
ATR1 uM combinations and other drugs in ATR1i combination as single agents

ATR1 uM combinations and other drugs in ATR1i combination as single agents 
Legend colors: red for more ck7 death
ED50 normalized by subtracting DMSO 
Pembro and PARPi single agent
Toxicity plot (CK7 dead/all cells - cd45 dead/all cells)
Pembro and PARPi single agent and toxicity plot for the same
ED75 normalized by subtracting DMSO 
Separate the STING plot into two: One plot containing samples S141, S147, S156 other with rest of the samples.
Toxicity plot?
Separate the STING plot into two: One plot containing samples S141, S147, S156 other with rest of the samples.

Therapy-resistant samples 

CK7+/All cells Ratio

Therapy-resistant samples 

gH2Ax+/All cells Ratio

gH2Ax+ (DNA damage marker)

Therapy-resistant samples 

gH2Ax +/CK7+ cells Ratio

Speaker notes:
What does this ratio really mean?

Number of cells with DNA damage/ number of cancer cells

HRD vs HRP slide /96 well samples

HRD vs HRP slide /96 well samples

ED75 (normalized by subtraction of DMSO 2 proportions)
The value in each cell is the proportion of images per treatment in which at least 75% of cancer cells are dead (CK7 pos and DCM pos) normalized by subtracting the proportion of images in control group in which at least 75% of cancer cells are dead 
Red squares correspond to greater cancer cell death after normalization

Toxicity plot ATRi
Toxicity ratio calculation per treatment and DMSO 2: CK7_toxicity_ratio = ((CK7+ dead - Double positive dead) - (CD45+ - Double positive dead))/all_cells
Log2FC value plotted is the log2 fold change of the treatment toxicity ratio divided by the control (DMSO 2) toxicity ratio

Toxicity plot PARPi and Pembro

ED50 PARPi and Pembro

Toxicity plot STINGa (Group 1 with higher STINGa concentrations)

ED75 STINGa (second half)

ED75 STINGa (second half)

Labels are wrong! 
1 = greater tumor cell death in comparison to control
0 = no significant change in tumor cell death
-1 = reduced tumor cell death in comparison to control

Methods (Raw images to Cell Profiler):

384 well plate (markers DCM nuclei staining, CK7 cell staining CD45 cell staining) → Imaging with Opera scanner (scanning takes 10-15 images of well and the microscope focuses on area in the well that have clumps of cells or spheroids)→ cell profiler (explanation of what cell profiler does; quantification of intensity values) → intensity values are outputted per cell (so each row of the csv file is per cell and information about the well and image number the cell is from are listed which we use later to group the cells by image and treatment) → using imageJ the intensity thresholds for DCM, CK7, and CD45 per patient

Methods (Cell Profiler to Script)

Explain all the steps (how we handled double positives, when is filtering outliers done, how did we decide the upper and lower limits for outliers, how did we calculate the ratios, how we handled Inf or NA values produced by the ratios, how did we normalize the data for Killing Efficiency
